media_title: How Not to Live Your Life

characters.name: Don
characters.description: Donald Danbury, more commonly known asDon, is a the protagonist in the television programme,How Not To Live Your Life, and he is portrayed by Dan Clark. Don is a neurotic, single twenty-nine year old, failing to navigate his way through the very basics of life. His biggest enemy is his overactive mind, which plays out countless scenes of things he shouldn't do or say. When he moves into a house left to him by his recently deceased grandmother, he meets Eddie, her over-enthusiastic carer who doesn't want to leave. Both Don's parents died at his birth. Don used to attendWestbrook SchoolwithAbby Joneswhom he had a crush on; he never had the courage to ask her out. During his time there he always used to wear a bandanna. When Abby and her family moved toManchester, Don thought about her once or twice a week. (TV:Home Sweet Home)Dave Fentimanwas his best friend at Westbrook. (TV:Don and the Wedding) When Don was 13 he did a charity run. At the age of 16 Don left school to pursue a life of fast living and good times. He never really had a dream as a child to be anything, and so skipped University. Don is a neurotic, single twenty-nine year old, failing to navigate his way through the very basics of life. His biggest enemy is his overactive mind, which plays out countless scenes of things he shouldn't do or say. When he moves into a house left to him by his recently deceased grandmother, he meets Eddie, her over-enthusiastic carer who doesn't want to leave.

When he moves into a house left to him by his recently deceased grandmother, he meets Eddie, her over-enthusiastic carer who doesn't want to leave.

characters.name: Eddie
characters.description: Edward Singh, more commonly known asEddie, is a fictional character in the television programme,How Not To Live Your Life, and he is portrayed by David Armand. Eddie was Don's grandmother's carer however, even after her death, he inexplicably continues to work for Don, caring for him in much the same way he'd care for an elderly client - doing housework, preparing meals and even offering Don sponge baths in bed. Throughout many episodes there are continuous gags that joke on the relationship between Eddie and Don mostly making them appear to be a gay couple, such as 'The Odd Couple'. His father is a sixteenth Indian, which Eddie reveals to Don who was confused about his surname. Eddie was Don's grandmother's carer however, even after her death, he inexplicably continues to work for Don, caring for him in much the same way he'd care for an elderly client - doing housework, preparing meals and even offering Don sponge baths in bed. Throughout many episodes there are continuous gags that joke on the relationship between Eddie and Don mostly making them appear to be a gay couple, such as 'The Odd Couple'. His father is a sixteenth Indian, which Eddie reveals to Don who was confused about his surname.

characters.name: Elsie Danbury
characters.description: Elsie DanburywasDonald Danbury's Nan. She considered him to be a dichead. Elsie was good friends withMrs. Treacher. (S1:Home Sweet Home)

characters.name: Mrs. Treacher
characters.description: Dorothy Treacher, more commonly known asDot, Mrs T, Gollum, Dobby or A Big Rat, is a fictional character in the television programme,How Not To Live Your Life, and she is portrayed by Leila Hoffman. Dot is Don's nosey next-door neighbour who refers to him as "dickhead". In series 2, Eddie has become her carer following her husband's death. She often tags along to events like Don's Gig in Episode 3. Dot is Don's nosey next-door neighbour who refers to him as "dickhead". In series 2, Eddie has become her carer following her husband's death. She often tags along to events like Don's Gig in Episode 3.
